overview: June first-pass summaries and the transposon update make the human relevance and claim boundaries clearer, but genomic instability still reads more like enabling biology than a mature intervention program.
next_stage_requirement: Controlled human evidence that genome-maintenance, retrotransposon, or somatic-mutation interventions hit their intended target or improve function.
key_question: Can tractable genome-maintenance interventions move human aging-relevant outcomes without unacceptable safety, mutation, or inflammation tradeoffs?
review_reason: June 2026 editorial rollup after the transposon and somatic mutation control missing-context publication added RTI biomarker and trial context, TPN-101 activity, DREAM biology, and TET2 CHIP boundaries without changing forecast status.

overview: Telomerase now has a human biomarker foothold, while telomere protection and capping remain animal-stage and safety-sensitive.
next_stage_requirement: Human biomarker or function data from clearly targeted telomere programs.
key_question: Can telomere-focused benefit be delivered without simply moving risk elsewhere?
review_reason: June 2026 editorial rollup after reviewed telomere restoration and protection baselines.

overview: Partial reprogramming still drives attention, while methylation and chromatin tracks keep the human-use story mixed and early.
next_stage_requirement: Safe human programs that go beyond clocks and short-term signatures.
key_question: Will epigenetic restoration stay measurable when the endpoint becomes actual human function?
review_reason: June 2026 editorial rollup after reviewed epigenetic restoration and chromatin baselines.

overview: The hallmark now spans animal proteasome work, protein-aggregate programs with real clinical endpoints, and a clearer chaperone signal: arimoclomol has NPC-specific human function and open-label context, but not a general aging result.
next_stage_requirement: Human programs that show interpretable aging-relevant functional endpoints outside narrow disease contexts, with clear safety and evidence that the intervention changed its intended target.
key_question: Can proteostasis support generalize beyond narrow disease programs, or will gains stay tied to specific proteins, cells, and indications?
review_reason: Reviewed after the June 11 Chaperone and Heat-Shock Support update added NPC open-label extension and current Miplyffa label safety context; this sharpened the track boundary without changing the hallmark-level read.

overview: Multiple autophagy-linked tracks now have human-relevant signals, and Autophagy Induction now includes a small older-adult spermidine vaccine-response pilot; target and immune-response changes still outrun long-lasting outcome evidence.
next_stage_requirement: Repeated human evidence that autophagy target changes or immune-response signals predict long-lasting functional, disease, or safety-relevant benefit.
key_question: Can changing autophagy in people produce long-lasting aging-relevant benefit rather than short-term biomarker or narrow immune-response movement?
review_reason: Reviewed after the June 11 Autophagy Induction surveillance publication added a small older-adult spermidine vaccine-response pilot while keeping the track tentative and subgroup-limited.

overview: This remains one of the most operationally mature hallmark areas, now with rapalog, AMPK, caloric-restriction-mimetic, and insulin/IGF baselines in view.
next_stage_requirement: Replicated human functional outcomes in older adults, not just pathway or biomarker movement.
key_question: Can the field turn mature biology into strong everyday gains in how people function?
review_reason: June 2026 editorial rollup after reviewed nutrient-sensing track baselines.

overview: The hallmark has several human functional or biomarker footholds, and NAD/redox now has more target-engagement context, but delivery and transplantation remain animal-led and long-lasting outcomes are inconsistent.
next_stage_requirement: More repeated, long-lasting human function evidence tied to specific mitochondrial programs.
key_question: Which mitochondrial signals actually predict healthy-lifespan gain rather than short-term pathway movement?
review_reason: Reviewed after the June 9 NAD/redox missing-context update; MIB-626 and no-result registry context widened the mitochondrial evidence picture without changing the hallmark summary.

overview: Senolytics now have the clearest early human biomarker story in this hallmark, while senomorphics and immune-clearance approaches still need stronger human outcome and safety evidence.
next_stage_requirement: Independent human studies showing long-lasting functional improvement, with acceptable safety.
key_question: Does clearing or modulating senescent cells move meaningful human outcomes enough to matter?
review_reason: Reviewed after the June 12 senolytics update added D+Q and fisetin biomarker, function, safety, animal, and no-results registry context while keeping the hallmark-level read early and safety-limited.

overview: Replacement and transplantation still carry the clearest human-functional case, while rejuvenation is now better bounded by a no-results HSC registry and review context; niche-support evidence remains indirect.
next_stage_requirement: Aging-relevant human endpoints that are not just narrow disease repair, no-results registry, or ex vivo cell stories.
key_question: Can regenerative capacity be restored in a way that generalizes beyond one tissue or indication?
review_reason: June 2026 editorial rollup after the stem-cell rejuvenation update added hDNAgr no-results registry context and a current HSC aging review without changing the hallmark read.

overview: Circulating factors, extracellular vesicles, and neuroendocrine reset now form a clearer track map, with S1PC/eNAMPT and klotho trial context sharpening the systems-signal branch.
next_stage_requirement: Sharper human programs with clearer signaling targets, controlled outcomes, and modality-specific safety.
key_question: Which systemic signals are causal, specific, and safe enough to justify targeted intervention?
review_reason: June 2026 editorial rollup after the neuroendocrine missing-context publication added S1PC/eNAMPT support and klotho trial boundaries without changing the track stage.

overview: Clinical outcome and immune-resilience signals keep this unusually legible, while resolution-pathway work remains more biomarker-level.
next_stage_requirement: Aging-specific interpretation of functional outcomes across populations and contexts.
key_question: Which anti-inflammatory gains are genuinely aging leverage rather than narrower disease management?
review_reason: June 2026 editorial rollup after reviewed inflammation and resolution-pathway baselines.

overview: The microbiome map is now broader: live biotherapeutics and metabolite restoration show narrow functional signals, while composition and ecosystem replacement remain bounded.
next_stage_requirement: Long-lasting outcome links that go beyond microbiome composition snapshots.
key_question: Which microbiome interventions matter because they change aging, not just because they change the microbiome?
review_reason: June 2026 editorial rollup after reviewed dysbiosis baselines and microbiome-composition surveillance.
